title: Southwest monsoon enters Odisha, brings relief from heatwave - News On AIR
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
full_text:
The southwest monsoon has entered Odisha, bringing much-needed relief from the prevailing heatwave conditions in the state.
According to the India Meteorological Department (IMD), the monsoon has advanced into several northern and coastal districts, including Jagatsinghpur, Kendrapara, Bhadrak, and Balasore.
The IMD has forecast light to moderate rainfall across many parts of the state till the 19th of this month. The showers are also expected to coincide with the three-day ‘Raja Festival’ celebrations, leading to a considerable drop in day temperatures that had crossed 40 degrees Celsius in several districts.
The timely arrival of the monsoon is expected to benefit the agricultural sector by facilitating the commencement of kharif sowing activities.
Meanwhile, the IMD has issued a yellow warning for various districts, cautioning people about the possibility of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty surface winds.

title: Southwest monsoon reaches Odisha, offering relief from heat - orissapost.com
full_text:
Bhubaneswar: Southwest monsoon arrived in Odisha Friday, ending a prolonged heatwave that had pushed temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius across several districts.
The India Meteorological Department said the monsoon advanced into northern and coastal districts including Jagatsinghpur, Kendrapara, Bhadrak and Balasore.
The IMD forecast light to moderate rainfall through June 19, with temperatures expected to drop considerably in the coming days.

The arrival coincides with the three-day Raja Festival, and forecasters said the rains are expected to support the start of kharif sowing — the summer crop season — benefiting farmers across the state.
The IMD issued a yellow warning for multiple districts, cautioning residents about possible thunderstorms, lightning and strong surface winds.
PNN
